expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: select a roadmap item; generate a draft spec prefilled with behaviors and rationale. Falsification: spec is Draft (not auto-Ready); cross-project roadmap items isolated.

playbook: Select a roadmap item; generate spec; verify prefilled content matches.
Combine 2 roadmap items into 1 spec; verify both are referenced.
Split 1 roadmap item into 2 specs; verify both reference the source.
Negative walks: cross-project.
nodes.rationale: Roadmap-to-spec is the bridge from craft-roadmap output to actionable work — distinct from manual / ticket creation.

nodes.title: Shape a draft spec for prioritization
nodes.scope: The shaping flow that brings a draft spec to a state where it can be evaluated for prioritization: clarifying questions answered, scope tight, boundaries with neighbors clear, behaviors named. Output is a 'shaped' spec — still Draft until acceptance criteria + dependencies are added (R-0077, R-0078) and ready-mark applied (R-0081).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: a shaped draft has tight scope, named behaviors, clear boundaries. Falsification: shaping does not auto-promote to Ready; shaping does not bypass acceptance criteria requirement.

playbook: Take a draft; complete shaping; verify the spec is annotated as 'shaped' and behaviors are named.
Verify state is still Draft.
Negative walk: shaping does not skip acceptance criteria.
nodes.rationale: Shaping is the prepare-to-prioritize step; distinct from acceptance criteria / dependencies / ready-marking.

nodes.title: Define acceptance criteria for a spec
nodes.scope: Add acceptance criteria to a spec — observable outcomes that must be true for the spec to be accepted. Each criterion ties to one or more accepted behaviors. Required before mark-ready.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: add criteria to a spec; each criterion linked to at least one behavior. Falsification: a criterion not linked to any behavior is flagged; mark-ready rejected without any criteria.

playbook: Add 2-3 criteria to a spec; verify behavior linkage.
Try mark-ready without criteria; verify rejection (this is the M-0008 readiness path).
Add a criterion without behavior link; verify warning.
nodes.rationale: Acceptance criteria are the foundation for behavior proof — distinct from shaping (R-0076) and dependencies (R-0078).

nodes.title: Declare dependencies for a spec
nodes.scope: Declare hard dependencies on other specs (within the same project). Dependencies prevent ready-state when unresolved. Cross-project dependencies are R-0079.
nodes.expected_offer: Single-project deps only here; cross-project in R-0079.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: declare deps on other specs; mark-ready rejected when unresolved. Falsification: cyclic dep rejected; declaring dep on a non-existent spec rejected.

playbook: Declare A depends on B (both Draft).
Try to mark A ready while B is not Done; verify rejection.
Mark B Done; verify A can be marked ready.
Negative walks: cyclic dep, non-existent dep.
nodes.rationale: Single-project deps have simpler semantics than cross-project (R-0079); split for proof clarity.

nodes.title: Honor cross-project spec dependencies
nodes.scope: Cross-project dependencies: a spec in project A can declare a dep on a spec in project B. The dep is one-way; B is unaffected by A's state. When B's spec is disconnected (R-0021), A's dep surfaces as unresolved.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: declare cross-project dep; resolution requires B-spec done; A waits. Falsification: B's project disconnect surfaces A's dep as unresolved; cyclic cross-project dep rejected.

playbook: Set up two projects; create specs in each; declare cross-project dep.
Verify A waits for B.
Disconnect B's project; verify A's dep surfaces as unresolved.
Negative walks: cycle, missing access.
nodes.rationale: Cross-project semantics carry their own invariants (visibility, disconnect surfacing) — distinct from single-project deps.

nodes.title: See a spec's current lifecycle state
nodes.scope: View a spec's current state across the lifecycle (Draft, Backlog, Ready, Blocked, Running, Awaiting walk, Done, Archived) plus any state-specific context (blockers if Blocked, walk progress if Awaiting walk, etc).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: view a spec; state is shown with state-specific context. Falsification: invalid state never appears; user without project visibility cannot view.

playbook: Per state, fixture a spec in that state; per interface, view; verify state + context.
Negative walks: cross-project visibility, invalid state injection.
nodes.rationale: State viewing is the read counterpart to all the lifecycle mutations (R-0081..R-0083); separate spec because the proof spans every state.

nodes.title: Mark a spec ready to run
nodes.scope: Transition a spec from Draft to Ready. Requires shaping (R-0076), acceptance criteria (R-0077), dependencies declared (R-0078), and dependencies resolved or known. Readiness gates from M-0008 also apply.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: a fully-shaped spec with criteria + deps moves to Ready. Falsification: missing any prerequisite blocks; readiness gates from M-0008 (clarifications, edge cases, oversize, untestable) block.

playbook: Shape a spec, add criteria + deps, deps resolved; mark ready; verify Ready state.
Try with missing pieces; verify each missing piece blocks with a clear reason.
Negative walk: M-0008 fixture (e.g. mark untestable); verify block.
nodes.rationale: Mark-ready is the gate that pulls in many requirements; its own spec because the proof has to exercise the prerequisite matrix.

nodes.title: Move a spec to or from the backlog
nodes.scope: Move a Ready spec to Backlog (deferred but eligible) and back to Ready. Backlog state preserves the spec's shape and criteria.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: Ready → Backlog → Ready works; spec content is preserved. Falsification: Running specs cannot be moved to Backlog; permission required.

playbook: Ready a spec; move to Backlog; verify state.
Move back to Ready; verify content preserved.
Negative walks: try with Running state; without permission.
nodes.rationale: Backlog transitions are simple but have permission and state-restriction invariants worth proving separately.

nodes.title: Archive a spec without implementation
nodes.scope: Archive a Draft / Backlog / Ready spec without implementing it. Archive preserves the spec's content and history but hides it from active views.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: archive a Draft / Backlog / Ready spec; content + history preserved; spec hidden from active views; can be reopened. Falsification: cannot archive Running / Awaiting walk; archive does not delete content.

playbook: Archive specs in each non-running state; verify history preservation.
Reopen one; verify it's restored to its prior state.
Negative walk: archive Running; expect rejection.
nodes.rationale: Archive is a soft-delete — its proof concerns differ from active mutations.

nodes.title: Group specs into a milestone
nodes.scope: Create milestones (named groupings of specs). Specs can belong to zero or one milestone; orphans are allowed. Milestones live within a project.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: create a milestone; assign specs to it; reassign / orphan specs. Falsification: spec belongs to at most one milestone; cross-project milestone rejected.

playbook: Create a milestone in a project.
Assign 3 specs to it; verify membership.
Move one to another milestone; verify it leaves the first.
Negative walks: cross-project milestone.
nodes.rationale: Milestone grouping is its own data model — distinct from spec lifecycle.

nodes.title: Group milestones into an initiative
nodes.scope: Initiatives are named groupings of milestones. Milestone can belong to zero or one initiative; orphan milestones allowed. Initiatives live within a project.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: create an initiative; group milestones; reassign / orphan. Falsification: milestone belongs to at most one initiative; cross-project initiative rejected.

playbook: Create an initiative; assign milestones; verify.
Move a milestone; orphan a milestone.
Negative walks: cross-project.
nodes.rationale: Same pattern as milestone grouping but at a higher level. Worth a separate spec because the data model and constraints differ slightly (one less containment level).

nodes.title: Perform bulk actions on multiple specs
nodes.scope: Apply an action (move to backlog, archive, assign to milestone, change owner) to a selection of specs at once. Selection is via filter or explicit list. Bulk actions are policy-gated and recorded in change history.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: select multiple specs; apply bulk action; all are updated. Positive: partial-failure cases preserve the failed specs unchanged with a clear report. Falsification: bulk action without permission rejected; bulk that would create cycles (e.g. cyclic deps) rejected.

playbook: Select 5 specs; bulk-archive; verify all archived.
Select 3 specs and bulk-assign to a milestone; verify.
Inject a failure on one spec; verify others succeed and the one is reported as failed.
Negative walks: bulk without permission; bulk that creates cycles.
nodes.rationale: Bulk actions have distinct invariants (atomicity, partial-failure semantics) worth proving separately.

nodes.title: Ask clarifying questions for vague work
nodes.scope: When shaping a spec, surface clarifying questions targeted at vagueness (missing user, missing outcome, ambiguous trigger). User can answer or defer. Unanswered required questions block ready-state.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: vague spec triggers clarifying questions; answering removes the questions; required-unanswered questions block ready. Falsification: questions don't auto-answer; deferring a required question doesn't bypass the readiness gate.

playbook: Author a vague spec; verify questions appear.
Answer required questions; verify they clear.
Try mark-ready with required-unanswered; verify rejection.
Negative walk: defer to bypass; expect rejection.
nodes.rationale: Clarifying questions are the entry-point of M-0008 — pre-implementation quality gate.

nodes.title: Identify missing edge cases in a spec
nodes.scope: Surface likely-missing edge cases (error paths, empty / max / overflow inputs, conflicting concurrent actions) for spec authors to consider. User accepts cases as in-scope, accepts as out-of-scope (with rationale), or defers.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: edge cases surfaced; user accepts / out-of-scopes; required edge cases block ready when not addressed. Falsification: out-of-scope without rationale flagged; auto-acceptance not allowed.

playbook: Author a spec; verify edge cases surface.
Accept some, out-of-scope some with rationale.
Try mark-ready with unaddressed required; verify rejection.
nodes.rationale: Edge case identification is distinct from clarifying questions (R-0087) — covers different vagueness dimensions.

nodes.title: Detect oversized specs and propose splits
nodes.scope: Detect when a spec is too large (covers too many behaviors, has unrelated outcomes, exceeds a sized heuristic) and propose split candidates. User can accept a proposed split, modify it, or override.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: an oversized spec triggers a split proposal; accepting creates child specs with proper linkage. Falsification: oversize warning does not silently split; user can override with rationale.

playbook: Author an oversized spec; verify proposal.
Accept the split; verify child specs created with linkage to parent.
Override with rationale on a different oversize; verify the override is recorded.
Negative walk: silent split rejected.
nodes.rationale: Split detection is the structural counterpart to scope-vagueness detection (R-0087, R-0088).

nodes.title: Block readiness for untestable behavior
nodes.scope: When a spec has acceptance criteria but no testable observable outcomes (or behaviors with no provable witness path), block mark-ready with a clear explanation. User must add testable criteria, accept the block, or rewrite.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: spec with untestable criteria blocks ready with a clear explanation. Falsification: testable spec is not blocked; bypass of the rule rejected.

playbook: Author a spec with vague / untestable criteria; try mark-ready; verify block + explanation.
Add testable criteria; verify ready works.
Negative walk: attempt to bypass via direct API; expect rejection.
nodes.rationale: Untestable-spec block is the bridge from M-0008 to M-0012 (behavior proof) — proves at this point that ready-state requires testability.

nodes.title: Explain why a spec is not ready
nodes.scope: Aggregate every reason a spec is not ready (missing criteria, missing deps, unresolved deps, unanswered questions, unaddressed edge cases, oversized, untestable) into a single explanation surface. User sees the full list and can address each.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: a not-ready spec shows every blocking reason. Positive: addressing reasons one by one updates the explanation. Falsification: explanation does not auto-clear without the underlying issue resolving; cannot mark ready while any block remains.

playbook: Compose a spec with multiple blockers; verify explanation lists all.
Address one at a time; verify updates.
Try mark-ready partway; verify rejection citing remaining blockers.
nodes.rationale: Aggregating block reasons is a meta-behavior — the user-facing surface of all M-0008 invariants. Worth its own spec because the aggregation correctness is itself testable.

nodes.title: Connect an organization-owned provider integration
nodes.scope: Org-tier provider connection (source control / CI / etc.) using org-owned credentials. Connection record carries provider id, capability set, owner scope, auth state, reachable resources.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: org admin connects a provider; record visible to org members; secrets not exposed. Falsification: non-admin cannot connect; tokens redacted in all read paths.

playbook: As org admin, connect a fixtured provider; verify the record appears with metadata only.
Verify org members can see the record; non-members cannot.
Negative: non-admin connects (rejected); secret value query returns redacted.
nodes.rationale: Org-tier and user-tier connect flows differ in scope, ownership, visibility — split for clean proof.

nodes.title: Connect a user-owned provider integration
nodes.scope: User-tier provider connection using user-owned credentials. Connection is private to the user's account.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: user connects a provider; record is private. Falsification: another user cannot see this connection; secret values not exposed.

playbook: As user A, connect a fixtured provider; verify the record is private.
As user B, attempt to view A's connection; verify rejection.
Negative: secret value query returns redacted.
nodes.rationale: User-owned mirrors R-0092 at user scope with stricter visibility.

nodes.title: Distinguish personal provider access from shared provider access
nodes.scope: Cross-tier visual / typed distinction between personal (R-0093) and shared (R-0092) provider access. Failure modes name the missing scope clearly.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: combined provider list shows personal vs shared with clear distinction. Falsification: shared access cannot be presented as personal.

playbook: Fixture personal + shared connections; per interface view the list; verify distinction.
Trigger work needing each kind; verify success / scope-named failure.
nodes.rationale: Distinguishability is its own surface invariant.

nodes.title: See provider connection health
nodes.scope: Per-connection health: live / degraded / failing / unconfigured, with last-checked timestamp. Active probe + passive cached error counts.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: healthy shows live; injected outage shows degraded within freshness window. Falsification: stale data marked, not silently treated as healthy.

playbook: Verify healthy = live.
Inject outage; verify degradation.
Stop probing; verify staleness marked.
nodes.rationale: Health visibility is the operator-visible side of provider connections.

nodes.title: See provider permissions and reachable resources
nodes.scope: Granted scopes / capabilities / reachable resources (repos, projects, workflows). Helps users assess sufficiency for intended work.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: connection shows granted permissions and reachable resources. Falsification: no secret tokens leaked; out-of-scope resources absent.

playbook: View a connection; verify permissions + resources.
Negative: confirm no token in payload; out-of-scope absent.
nodes.rationale: Permission visibility is the precondition for B-0228 recovery and for ongoing audit.

nodes.title: Recover from provider authorization failure
nodes.scope: Auth failure surfaces with cause + guided recovery (re-auth / scope-up / re-connect). Affected work surfaces an actionable error.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: fixtured auth failure surfaces with cause + recovery; recovery restores connection. Falsification: silent retry with stale auth rejected.

playbook: Inject fixtured token revocation; verify surface.
Walk recovery; verify connection restored.
Negative: silent retry rejected.
nodes.rationale: Auth failure is the most common provider problem; explicit recovery is its own behavior.

nodes.title: Audit external integration actions
nodes.scope: Audit log of every action Tanren took through a provider: who triggered, what was attempted, outcome, response metadata (no secret values).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: actions appear in the audit log with attribution + timestamp. Falsification: secret values absent; non-authorized users cannot view.

playbook: Trigger fixtured actions through both connection types; verify log entries.
Inspect entries; verify no secret values.
Negative: non-authorized user view rejected.
nodes.rationale: External-action audit is a discrete read-only surface.

nodes.title: Set up harness credentials, project harness selection, and see harness readiness
nodes.scope: Bundled harness setup: add credentials (B-0099), choose project harnesses (B-0100), see readiness (B-0101). Sequential setup steps with shared UI plumbing.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: add credentials; metadata-only display; usable by selected harnesses. Falsification: secret values redacted.
Per interface — Positive: project admin selects allowed harnesses; selection persists. Falsification: non-admin cannot change; cannot exceed organization-allowed.
Per interface — Positive: readiness shows live for ready harnesses, blocked with reason for not-ready. Falsification: stale readiness marked stale.

playbook: Add credentials; select harnesses; view readiness.
Negatives: missing creds, scope violations, stale data.
nodes.rationale: Sequential setup steps with shared UI; bundled to reduce scaffolding.

nodes.title: Start work in an isolated execution environment
nodes.scope: Dispatch a unit of work into a sandboxed target with isolated workspace + configured harness.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: dispatched work runs in an isolated target with a clean workspace. Falsification: cross-target data leakage rejected.

playbook: Dispatch fixtured task; verify isolated workspace.
Dispatch a second; verify no cross-contamination.
nodes.rationale: Isolation is the core runtime property.

nodes.title: See where active work runs
nodes.scope: View execution location of in-flight work: target id, target type, provider account, region.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: in-flight shows location. Falsification: completed no longer advertised; sensitive infra detail not leaked.

playbook: Dispatch; verify location.
Wait for completion; verify archived but not advertised live.
Negative: sensitive infra omitted.
nodes.rationale: Where-running visibility is the transparency surface for runtime.

nodes.title: See execution environment access
nodes.scope: View target's scoped credentials, allowed network egress, allowed FS, harness identity. Helps users understand security boundary.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: view shows scoped creds, egress, identity. Falsification: secret values redacted; access list does not leak hidden resources.

playbook: View in-flight target's access; verify scope match.
Negatives: secrets, hidden resources.
nodes.rationale: Access transparency is a security/audit surface.

nodes.title: Stop or recover interrupted execution
nodes.scope: User stop or system recovery for interrupted work. State recorded; recovered work continues from known state or asks user.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: stop / recover work as expected. Falsification: no duplicate visible work; worker session reconciliation linked.

playbook: Stop running unit.
Simulate crash; verify recovery flow.
Verify no duplicate visible work.
nodes.rationale: Recovery is a complex state machine — its own spec.

nodes.title: See harness-neutral runtime failure source signals
nodes.scope: Failures inside a target surface with neutral classification (timeout / network / harness internal / runtime crash / target unavailable).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: each fixtured failure class surfaces correctly. Falsification: harness-specific stack traces not exposed by default; secrets not in payloads.

playbook: Inject each class; verify classification.
Inspect payload; verify redaction.
nodes.rationale: Harness-neutral failure classification is a key UX property.

nodes.title: Retry transient runtime failures without duplicating visible work
nodes.scope: Auto-retry on transient classes; uses same assignment / spec; user sees attempts but no duplicate visible work. Pairs with R-0115, which owns the worker-side dedup invariant that makes user-visible retry safe.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: transient failure auto-retries; success emerges. Falsification: retry never creates duplicate; permanent failures do not auto-retry.

playbook: Inject transient; verify retry + success.
Inject permanent; verify no auto-retry.
Negative: no duplicate work.
nodes.rationale: Transient retry is the runtime's automation.
nodes.boundary_with_neighbors: User-visible retry capability for transient runtime failures. The non-duplication invariant is owned by R-0115 (B-0250); this node consumes that invariant when surfacing retry to the user. Failure-source attribution is R-0104; harness-neutral runtime failure surfacing is R-0118.

nodes.title: Manage VM or remote execution targets
nodes.scope: Register / view / update / remove VM or remote targets. Targets have name, scoped capabilities, connected creds, identity.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: register / view / update / remove a target. Falsification: non-admin cannot manage; conflicting target id rejected.

playbook: Register, view, update, remove fixtured target.
Negatives: permission, duplicate id.
nodes.rationale: Target management is operator-tier.

nodes.title: Retire or drain an execution target
nodes.scope: Mark target draining (no new work) or retired (terminate). Active work completes or migrates per policy.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: drain stops new dispatch; retire after drain removes target. Falsification: retire with active work without explicit force rejected.

playbook: Drain target with active work; verify dispatch goes elsewhere.
Wait completion; retire.
Negatives: force-retire surfaced as warning.
nodes.rationale: Drain semantics are non-trivial.

nodes.title: Grant or revoke worker-scoped temporary access
nodes.scope: Issue short-lived credentials scoped to one assignment; revoke when ended / cancelled. Bundled because grant + revoke share lifecycle.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: grant scoped access; works within scope. Falsification: out-of-scope use rejected; expired access rejected.
Per interface — Positive: revoke; subsequent use rejected. Falsification: revocation cannot be silently undone.

playbook: Grant scoped access; verify in-scope works, out-of-scope fails.
Revoke; verify rejection.
Negative: silent re-enable rejected.
nodes.rationale: Grant + revoke share lifecycle scaffolding.

nodes.title: Manage cloud or VM provider accounts
nodes.scope: Operator manages connections to cloud / VM providers (AWS / GCP / etc.) used to provision execution targets.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: connect cloud account; create / drain / retire targets via it. Falsification: non-operator cannot manage; secrets not exposed.

playbook: Connect fixtured cloud account.
Create target via account.
Negatives: permission, secret exposure.
nodes.rationale: Cloud-provider management is operator-tier.

nodes.title: Receive a scoped worker assignment
nodes.scope: Worker contract entry-point: agent-worker receives an assignment with scope (spec, phase, harness, credentials, target, timeout).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: well-formed assignment received; envelope decoded. Falsification: malformed rejected; missing required fields rejected.

playbook: Send well-formed assignment; verify receipt.
Send malformed; verify rejection.
nodes.rationale: Inbound entry of the worker contract.

nodes.title: Refuse invalid or unauthorized worker assignments
nodes.scope: Worker explicitly refuses assignments outside capability / authorization. Refusal carries reason code + detail.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: invalid assignments produce structured refusal. Falsification: silent acceptance of out-of-scope rejected.

playbook: Send out-of-capability; verify refusal.
Send expired-credential; verify refusal.
Negative: silent acceptance rejected.
nodes.rationale: Refusal is the safety contract.

nodes.title: Report worker progress, submit results, and report blockers
nodes.scope: Three worker-to-coordinator report types: progress (durable, periodic), results (at phase completion), blockers (pause + structured question). Shared envelope and idempotency.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: progress persists durably; survives coordinator restart. Falsification: progress not silently dropped on retry.
Per interface — Positive: results submitted appear with attribution; spec advances. Falsification: results from unauthorized worker rejected.
Per interface — Positive: blocker pauses + surfaces with options. Falsification: blocker without structured options rejected.

playbook: Report progress periodically.
Submit results at phase end.
Report blocker with options.
nodes.rationale: Three report types share contract — bundled to avoid replicated scaffolding.

nodes.title: Report worker terminal outcome
nodes.scope: Worker reports final outcome (success / failure / cancelled / abandoned). Outcome closes the assignment lifecycle.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: terminal closes lifecycle; subsequent reports rejected. Falsification: terminal cannot be reported twice.

playbook: Report success.
Try second terminal; verify rejection.
Negative: re-open after close rejected.
nodes.rationale: Lifecycle close-out.

nodes.title: Honor worker cancellation and access revocation
nodes.scope: On cancel / revoke, worker stops + reports + releases. Worker that ignores is force-cancelled.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: cancel acknowledged; worker stops within timeout. Falsification: misbehaving worker force-cancelled and reported.

playbook: Cancel; verify worker stops.
Test misbehavior; verify force-cancel.
nodes.rationale: Critical contract invariant.

nodes.title: Avoid duplicate visible work on retry
nodes.scope: Retried work links to original assignment / work item; specs / PRs / reviews / ownership not duplicated. Worker-side contract obligation. Pairs with R-0105, which surfaces the user-visible retry capability that depends on this invariant.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: retry produces no duplicate visible work. Falsification: silent duplication rejected.

playbook: Force retry; verify no duplicate.
Verify each attempt visible with reason.
nodes.rationale: Worker-side duplicate-avoidance contract.
nodes.boundary_with_neighbors: Worker-side dedup invariant: a retried unit of work yields one set of visible side effects regardless of how many retry attempts occur. R-0105 (B-0107) is the user-facing retry surface that depends on this invariant. Cross-references R-0153 (retry-safe / replay-safe client request acceptance).

nodes.title: Use only granted worker access
nodes.scope: Worker uses only granted creds / env access; out-of-scope rejected and reported. Cross-spec leakage prevention.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: in-scope access works. Falsification: out-of-scope denied; cross-spec creds rejected; misbehaving worker reported.

playbook: In-scope works.
Out-of-scope denied + reported.
Cross-spec leak rejected.
nodes.rationale: Scope discipline is a security invariant.

nodes.title: Preserve worker output without leaking secrets
nodes.scope: Worker outputs persisted with explicit redaction; redaction is reviewable.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: output preserved with redaction. Falsification: no secret values in persisted log; redaction explicit.

playbook: Worker emits output with fixtured secret values; verify redaction.
Inspect persisted record; verify explicit redaction marker.
nodes.rationale: Secret redaction is critical.

nodes.title: Distinguish worker / harness / runtime / provider failure
nodes.scope: Worker reports failures attributed to right layer (worker logic / harness / runtime / external provider). Drives retry behavior + analysis.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: each class attributed correctly. Falsification: misattribution rejected; opaque 'unknown' rare.

playbook: Inject failure at each layer; verify attribution.
Inject opaque; verify probe data captured.
nodes.rationale: Failure attribution drives all downstream recovery.

nodes.title: Resume or reconcile interrupted worker sessions
nodes.scope: Interrupted session resumes via reconciliation: re-establish identity, fetch last-acked state, continue. Coordinator brokers safely.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: simulated interruption; reconciled session resumes from last ack. Falsification: stale-token resume rejected; cross-worker reconcile rejected.

playbook: Run worker; interrupt; reconcile; verify resume from last ack.
Negatives: stale token, cross-worker reconcile.
nodes.rationale: Runtime-actor counterpart to R-0103.

nodes.title: Start an implementation loop manually or automatically

Per interface — Positive: serial policy auto-starts next Ready spec. Falsification: parallel policy disables; manual policy requires explicit start.

playbook: Manual: Ready → start → Running.
Auto-start: configure serial; verify next Ready auto-starts.
Negatives: non-ready, permission, policy off.

nodes.title: See current state of an implementation loop

Confirm staleness marker; cross-project check.

nodes.title: Respond to a question when a loop pauses on a blocker
nodes.scope: When loop pauses on a blocker (B-0247), surface structured question with options and resume on response.

Pick one; verify resume.
Negative: invalid option, silent resume.

nodes.title: Trigger a walk for implementation-ready work

Verify notification per preferences.

playbook: Pause; verify dispatch stops.
Resume; verify continuation.
Negatives: invalid transitions.
nodes.rationale: Operator control distinct from blocker pause.

nodes.title: Inspect detailed live activity of a running loop
nodes.scope: Drill-down stream of recent events, worker progress, file changes, gate runs, with redaction.

playbook: Stream fixtured loop's activity; verify ordering + redaction.
Negatives: cross-project, secret leak.
nodes.rationale: Streaming surface distinct from summary state.

nodes.title: Block starting a loop when declared dependencies are not usable
nodes.scope: Pre-start check: every declared dep must be usable. Block with reason if not.

Mark Done; start; verify success.
Negative: bypass rejected.
nodes.rationale: Dep enforcement at start time.

nodes.title: Cancel a loop
nodes.scope: User cancels running loop; worker honors; spec moves to cancelled with attribution; partial work preserved per project policy.

playbook: Cancel fixtured running loop; verify worker stop.
Negatives: permission, invalid state.
nodes.rationale: User-driven lifecycle terminator.

nodes.title: Coordinate candidate implementations for one spec
nodes.scope: Multiple candidate impls per spec: track each as own attempt, surface comparable outcomes, choose one to advance.

Pick one; verify advance + others abandoned.
Negative: cross-candidate stomp.

nodes.title: Run a stacked-diff dependent spec against an available base

Merge A; verify B rebases.
Abandon A; verify B surfaces error.

nodes.title: Produce executable behavior proof
nodes.scope: Author and run BDD scenarios for accepted behaviors. Each scenario tags its behavior id with positive or falsification witness. Asserted requires both witness types passing.

Run; verify both pass.
Mark asserted; verify policy check.
Negative: missing falsification → cannot mark.

nodes.title: See unresolved check findings that block readiness
nodes.scope: Findings UI: every finding (gates / audit / adherence / demo / proof) appears with severity, scope, resolution state. Readiness gate consumes this state.

Resolve one; verify history.
Verify mark-ready respects findings.

nodes.title: Run a codebase-wide audit

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: audit completes and emits findings. Falsification: honors permissions; no secret values in findings.

playbook: Run audit; verify findings emit.
Negatives: permission, secret leak.

nodes.title: Review delivered behavior during a walk

playbook: Open walk session for fixtured Awaiting-walk spec.
Negatives: missing proof, cross-project.
nodes.rationale: Walk session start surface.

nodes.title: Present walk demo summary, expected vs actual, and changed surfaces
nodes.scope: Walk content presentation: demo summary (B-0166), expected vs actual (B-0167), changed-surfaces and residual-risk (B-0168). Three views in one walk session.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: demo summary names accepted behaviors + outcomes + source signals. Falsification: missing source signals visible; demo cannot bypass missing items.
Per interface — Positive: expected next to observed; differences flagged. Falsification: missing observed marked, not faked.
Per interface — Positive: changed surfaces listed; risks visible; risk acceptance routing. Falsification: unknown risk not silently zero.

playbook: Open walk; verify three views.
Per view, fixture content variants.
Negatives: missing-data masking, cross-project leak.
nodes.rationale: Three views in one session — bundled scaffolding.

nodes.title: Preserve walk acceptance record after the walk
nodes.scope: Record walk outcome with attribution + timestamp + source signals. Persists through merge / cleanup / replan.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: record persists through merge / cleanup / replan. Falsification: redacted source signals show hidden material existed.

playbook: Walk to completion; verify record.
Merge work; verify record persists.
Replan over; verify still accessible.
nodes.rationale: Durability invariant.

nodes.title: Accept walked work
nodes.scope: Accept the walked work. Spec moves toward merge-ready; subsequent code changes invalidate prior acceptance unless reaffirmed.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: accept; spec progresses; attribution. Falsification: cannot accept without proof; further code changes invalidate stale acceptance.

playbook: Accept fixtured walk.
Push code change after acceptance; verify staleness.
Negative: accept without proof.

nodes.title: Reject walked work and route follow-up work
nodes.scope: Reject with structured follow-up routing (new spec / blocker / more proof). Routing explicit.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: reject with routing; spec returns to working. Falsification: rejection without routing rejected; routing without rationale rejected.

Reject with blocker routing.
Negatives: no routing, missing rationale.
nodes.rationale: Decision flow distinct from acceptance.

nodes.title: Create a pull request from walked work

playbook: Accept and create PR via fixtured provider; verify linkage.
Inject provider failure; verify surface.
Negative: PR without acceptance.

nodes.title: See pull request and CI state from the spec
nodes.scope: Spec-context view of PR + CI: PR status, reviewer state, CI checks, latest commit. Pulled from provider integration.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: PR + CI state visible from spec; updates in freshness window. Falsification: stale data marked; secrets not exposed.

playbook: Open spec view with PR; verify state.
Trigger CI failure; verify update.
Negatives: staleness, secret leak.

nodes.title: Route review feedback into follow-up work and mark addressed or out-of-scope
nodes.scope: Route review feedback into a follow-up task; mark addressed (linked code change) or out-of-scope (with rationale).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: feedback creates follow-up tied to spec. Falsification: silent dismissal rejected.
Per interface — Positive: mark addressed with link; mark out-of-scope with rationale. Falsification: out-of-scope without rationale rejected.

Mark another addressed with link.
Mark another out-of-scope with rationale.

nodes.title: Merge accepted work

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: merge with CI green + acceptance + approvals; spec → Done. Falsification: failing CI rejected; without acceptance rejected; without approvals rejected.

playbook: Set up CI green + acceptance + approvals; merge.
Negatives: failing CI, missing acceptance, missing approval.

nodes.title: Clean up completed execution resources
nodes.scope: Post-completion cleanup: targets returned, branches archived, ephemeral worker access revoked. Permanent records preserved.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: ephemeral resources removed; permanent records preserved. Falsification: cleanup deleting permanent record rejected.

Verify proof / walk / audit preserved.
Negative: cleanup deleting permanent rejected.
nodes.rationale: Operator-tier responsibility distinct from merge.

nodes.title: Recover from merge conflicts after parallel work lands
nodes.scope: Merge conflict surfaces; routed to actor (worker for code, human for intent); resume merge after resolution.

Resolve via worker; verify re-merge.
Negative: silent merge rejected.
nodes.rationale: Sub-flow distinct from merge.

nodes.title: Detect intent conflicts after related work lands
nodes.scope: Detect higher-level intent conflicts (similar features differently, contradicting outcomes) post-merge. Surface for human review.

Resolve; verify recorded.
Negative: silent acceptance.
nodes.rationale: Distinct post-merge analysis behavior.

nodes.title: Configure organization-tier policies (placement, harness allowlist, budget, standards)
nodes.scope: Authoring + applying organization-tier policies that constrain runtime placement (B-0081), allowed harnesses (B-0082), budget and quota (B-0083), and standards (B-0084). Edits are policy-gated and recorded; constraints flow into runtime, harness, and standards systems.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: org admin sets placement policy; runtime decisions respect it. Falsification: bypass via project tier rejected when policy locks.
Per interface — Positive: harness allowlist set; project's allowed set respects it. Falsification: project cannot exceed org allowlist.
Per interface — Positive: budget / quota set; over-limit work paused. Falsification: bypass rejected.
Per interface — Positive: standards policy set; project standards conform. Falsification: forbidden override rejected.

Test inheritance + override via policy lock.
Negatives: bypass attempts.

nodes.title: Configure project policy inheritance and overrides

nodes.rationale: Inheritance semantics distinct from policy authoring.

nodes.title: Manage and apply organization standards profiles

Per interface — Positive: apply profile; project shows origin per standard. Falsification: missing profile rejected; project edits to profile-sourced standards explicit.

nodes.title: Connect, disconnect, or replace an external tracker

Per interface — Positive: disconnect or replace; references show as unresolved or migrate. Falsification: cannot delete external tickets.

Replace; verify migration.
Disconnect; verify unresolved refs.

nodes.title: See unshaped external tickets
nodes.scope: List of tracker tickets not yet turned into Tanren specs.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: list shows unshaped tickets. Falsification: shaped tickets do not appear; cross-project blocked.

Shape one; verify it leaves.

nodes.title: See outbound issues and review queued ones before push

nodes.title: Configure outbound issue review mode

nodes.title: See external reference state and quick-access links from a spec

nodes.title: See coherent Tanren state across public interfaces
nodes.scope: State produced on one public interface is observable on every other public interface within an explicit freshness window. Freshness window: explicit-fetch reads (api/mcp/cli direct queries) converge in ≤ 2 s; passive views (web dashboards, tui live panes) converge in ≤ 30 s after the originating mutation commits. Cover the canonical mutating actions (auth state change; project create or disconnect; spec lifecycle transitions; loop start / pause / cancel; walk acceptance; merge or revert) and assert that each is observable on every other interface within the window. Includes a falsification case proving that forced divergence is detected and not silently masked.

expected_evidence.description: Cross-interface coherence within the declared freshness window. Freshness window: explicit-fetch reads (api/mcp/cli direct queries) converge in ≤ 2 s; passive views (web dashboards, tui live panes) converge in ≤ 30 s after the originating mutation commits. For each canonical mutation source listed below, assert: Positive: the resulting state is observable on every other interface within the window. Falsification: an injected divergence (state on one interface out of sync past the window) is surfaced as an error rather than silently masked. Mutation sources: auth state change (sign-in / sign-out); project create or disconnect; spec lifecycle transition (draft → ready, ready → archived); loop start / pause / cancel; walk acceptance; merge or revert.

playbook: Auth state change: sign in via web; verify session is reflected on api, mcp, cli, tui within the freshness window. Repeat with sign-out.
Project create / disconnect: create a project via api; verify visibility on web, mcp, cli, tui. Disconnect via cli; verify removal on others.
Spec lifecycle: mark a spec ready via mcp; verify the ready state on web, api, cli, tui. Archive via tui; verify on others.
Loop start / pause / cancel: start a loop via cli; verify loop state on web, api, mcp, tui. Pause via web; verify on others. Cancel via api; verify on others.
Walk acceptance: accept a walked spec via web; verify acceptance on api, mcp, cli, tui.
Merge / revert: merge accepted work via api; verify post-merge state on web, mcp, cli, tui. Revert via cli; verify on others.

Falsification: force a divergence (e.g. by stalling one interface's projection) past the freshness window; assert the affected interface surfaces a staleness error rather than silently serving stale state.
nodes.rationale: Foundational guarantee for multi-interface.
nodes.boundary_with_neighbors: R-0200 owns user-driven session continuity (resume same work on another interface). R-0201 owns shared validation-error semantics. This node owns the freshness/convergence guarantee itself.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: feedback captured + linked. Falsification: cross-project leakage blocked.

playbook: Submit post-ship feedback; verify capture + linking.
nodes.rationale: Post-ship intake.

nodes.title: Update roadmap and specs from post-ship outcomes
nodes.scope: Outcomes (success metrics, regressions, feedback themes) feed into roadmap revisions / new spec proposals.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: outcome triggers roadmap-update proposal. Falsification: not silently applied.

playbook: Inject outcome; verify proposal.
Approve via R-0069.
nodes.rationale: Outcomes-to-roadmap learning loop.

nodes.title: Configure autonomy level
nodes.scope: Per-project / per-org setting controlling Tanren's autonomy level: full / approval-gated / fully-manual.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: set level; downstream actions honor. Falsification: change without permission rejected.

playbook: Set each level; verify downstream.
nodes.rationale: Master config for the M-0024 cluster.

nodes.title: Require approval before sensitive actions
nodes.scope: Sensitive actions pause for explicit user approval per autonomy level.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: action pauses + waits for approval. Falsification: does not proceed without approval.

playbook: Trigger sensitive action; verify pause.
Approve.
nodes.rationale: Human-in-the-loop guarantee.

nodes.title: Let low-risk work continue until a blocker
nodes.scope: Low-risk work flows without approval until sensitive action / blocker surfaces.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: low-risk flows; sensitive interrupts. Falsification: sensitive does not silently flow.

playbook: Run low-risk; verify flow.
Inject sensitive; verify pause.
nodes.rationale: Low-risk-flow distinct from approval-required.

nodes.title: Stop automation at user-set boundaries
nodes.scope: Hard boundaries (e.g. 'never auto-merge to main') halt automation regardless of autonomy level.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: boundary halts automation. Falsification: cannot silently bypass.

playbook: Declare boundary; trigger crossing; verify halt.
nodes.rationale: Hard boundaries override autonomy.

nodes.title: Approve a generated plan when policy requires approval
nodes.scope: Generated plan revision + policy-required approval: user sees rationale + impact, approves / rejects.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: generated plan with rationale; approve applies. Falsification: does not apply without approval.

playbook: Trigger plan generation under policy.
Approve / reject.
nodes.rationale: Plan approval is specific gating in autonomy cluster.

nodes.title: See gated action approval state and collect required approvals
nodes.scope: See pending approvals (B-0197) + collect them from permitted approvers (B-0198).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: pending shows required + status. Falsification: stale state marked.
Per interface — Positive: collect from permitted; threshold; action proceeds. Falsification: outside permitted set rejected.

playbook: View pending.
Collect approvals; verify proceed.
nodes.rationale: Approval visibility + collection paired.

nodes.title: Compare candidate work and recommend roadmap sequencing with tradeoffs
nodes.scope: Compare candidate roadmap items (B-0158) and recommend sequencing with tradeoffs (B-0159).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: comparison shows scope, value, risk, deps. Falsification: invalid rejected.
Per interface — Positive: recommendation shows tradeoffs. Falsification: no recommendation without sufficient signal.

playbook: Compare 3 candidates.
Get recommendation.
nodes.rationale: Comparison + recommendation paired.

nodes.title: Defer work with an explicit rationale
nodes.scope: Move roadmap item to deferred state with mandatory rationale + revisit-by date.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: defer with rationale + revisit; resurfaces. Falsification: defer without rationale rejected.

playbook: Defer with rationale; advance time; verify resurface.
nodes.rationale: Deferral with rationale prevents silent dropping.

nodes.title: Rebalance the roadmap after new source signals
nodes.scope: Periodic / triggered rebalancing pass: incorporate new signals; propose revised sequencing.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: rebalance produces proposal with rationale. Falsification: not silently applied.

playbook: Inject signals; rebalance; verify proposal.
nodes.rationale: Active maintenance loop on roadmap.

nodes.title: Restore a previous revision without deleting history
nodes.scope: Restore prior revision; restoration is itself a new revision; intervening history preserved.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: restore preserves history. Falsification: cross-project blocked.

playbook: Edit, edit, restore to first; verify history.
nodes.rationale: Durable-undo guarantee.

nodes.title: Revert landed work through controlled follow-up
nodes.scope: Revert through follow-up spec (normal review / approval / merge). No silent reverts.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: revert via follow-up spec; normal flow. Falsification: silent revert rejected.

playbook: Trigger revert; verify follow-up; walk through flow.
nodes.rationale: Revert through normal channel preserves accountability.

nodes.title: Recover from a bad planning decision without losing history
nodes.scope: Record superseding decision; original preserved with reasoning chain.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: superseding decision recorded; original preserved. Falsification: not silently deleted.

playbook: Identify bad decision; record superseding; verify both preserved.
nodes.rationale: Decision-recovery distinct from artifact restoration.

nodes.title: Capture human-authored product signals as candidate work
nodes.scope: Capture customer feedback, meeting notes, stakeholder requests with source / context; defer / link / candidate-spec.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: capture; defer / link / candidate-spec. Falsification: not auto-accepted; cross-project blocked.

playbook: Capture; defer.
Capture; turn into candidate.
Capture; link to existing spec.
nodes.rationale: Human-signal intake with explicit review.

nodes.title: Turn audit findings into specs or backlog items
nodes.scope: Audit finding to candidate spec / backlog with finding as context. User reviews + accepts / dismisses.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: finding to candidate; accept advances. Falsification: not silently advanced.

playbook: Trigger finding; turn into candidate; accept.
nodes.rationale: Finding-to-spec bridge.

nodes.title: Classify bug reports against behavior status
nodes.scope: Classify bug report against catalog: regression / unmet implemented / proposed new / out of scope. Drives routing.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: classified with rationale. Falsification: reviewable + correctable.

playbook: Submit bug; verify classification.
Correct.
nodes.rationale: Bug-classification drives routing.

nodes.title: Route proactive analysis into planning
nodes.scope: From any proactive analysis output (mutation, security, perf, deps), route findings into planning as candidates.

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: analysis triggers candidate. Falsification: no silent acceptance.

playbook: Inject analysis output; verify routing.
nodes.rationale: Umbrella for proactive findings into planning.

nodes.title: Schedule, run, and review proactive analysis recommendations
nodes.scope: Schedule recurring / on-demand analyses (B-0286) + review recommendations before planning entry (B-0287).

expected_evidence.description: Per interface — Positive: schedule + on-demand. Falsification: respects permissions.
Per interface — Positive: recommendations reviewable. Falsification: not silently advanced.

playbook: Schedule; run on-demand; review.
nodes.rationale: Schedule + review paired in analysis lifecycle.
